title: Why isnt kensuke a more popular character
display_title: Why isnt kensuke a more popular character
wikitext:
[]: Why isn't kensuke a more popular character             Quote Post by hwdge » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:35 pm The guy's hilarious and not nearly as dysfunctional as other people

[]:             Quote Post by Rena » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:52 pm Maybe it's the fact he blindly wants to be an Eva pilot, and can't see how much pain the pilots go through? He also wasn't that in-depth a character, in my opinion. Onscreen, he's easily summarised as "Military Otaku, friends with Shinji and Toji, likes Misato, wants to pilot Eva badly". I'll admit, I did find him funny at times, but I thought his reasons to want to pilot were really shallow.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:58 pm School-centric fanfictions commonly give him a lot of attention as one of "The six" - him, Rei, Shinji, Asuka, Toji, Hikari. When they involve robots, they often place him in Unit 04, 05, or 06. That's quite a lot of attention for someone who basically didn't exist from episode 5 onward.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:15 pm He's a weak supporting character that only gets some actual development in episode four. Otherwise, he's just comic relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Mar 28, 2013 8:53 pm While i'm not exactly Kensuke's biggest fan, i must admit that Kensuke has much more value and insight than he's often given credit for. While it's true that his interests and attitudes seem single-minded and superficial, the few times he does give an advise or otherwise important contribution, the lucidity and insight of his statements can be comparable to those of Kaworu. I actually wouldn't be surprised if some of his statement were conceived as a commentary on the very raison'd'etre or nature of NGE. Sorry if this is all too short or vague, kind of tired at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Mar 28, 2013 9:32 pm Dream wrote: While i'm not exactly Kensuke's biggest fan, i must admit that Kensuke has much more value and insight than he's often given credit for. While it's true that his interests and attitudes seem single-minded and superficial, the few times he does give an advise or otherwise important contribution, the lucidity and insight of his statements can be comparable to those of Kaworu. I actually wouldn't be surprised if some of his statement were conceived as a commentary on the very raison'd'etre or nature of NGE. Sorry if this is all too short or vague, kind of tired at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by EVAfacepalm » Thu Mar 28, 2013 10:19 pm Character details aside, it could also be that most peoples' first experience was the dub. Kensuke's voice in the dub is horrible . So to most people Kensuke is identified as "that guy with the weird voice that sounds like... (insert reference here, I can't think of anything that resembles dub Kensuke's voice).

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Thu Mar 28, 2013 11:34 pm I thought Kensuke gets the reputation on being that one kid who will never pilot an eva. I recall he dose get selected as a pilot in one game only for it to blow up somehow(there were no angels, so it must have been something completely different). I think his interest on the military gives him better insight on life and death situations mixed in with the immaturity of a kid. However, I do believe that the Shinji in Toji's and Kensuke's mind is that Shinji is some season trained pilot like Asuka. However he was just unlucky with no training experience. If Toji knew that, he wouldn't have punched him and would blame Nerv rather than Shinji. However, I did dislike him because I misinterpret the last line he says to Shinji which made him come off as a selfish duche not caring about what happened to Toji. Thou it could have been possible that he doesn't know what happened to Toji. Also, I do think he is the best choice to have at the cockpit of an Eva. Since he dose have the enthusiasm and being a military fanboy dose give him a better perspective on life and death situations as well as knowledge on weaponry and tactics.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Fri Mar 29, 2013 3:18 am I always see Kensuke and Toji as a pair, where Kensuke's cool intellect offsets Toji's emotional brashness (and Toji's more grounded personality helps restrain Kensuke's more flighty imaginary life). Indeed at the least their names are both taken from Ai to Gensou no Fascism . Like a lot of things in EVA I get the impression that with the other students in Class 2-A they were laying the foundation for a much more epic series and it's never really been fully realized. I imagine if they ever run out of story ideas with the pilot trio that there's more story behind Kensuke, Toji, and Hikari that they could pull out. When it comes to the EVAs Kensuke always does seem to get the short end of the stick. He never gets a chance in the original series despite volunteering for the job. In the game GOS2 he's assigned to EVA04 and even gets a plug suit, but he never gets to pilot it. It seems its sort of a running joke among GAINAX that the most military-minded of the students never gets to actually fight. I actually have him as a pilot later on in my Blue Rose series of FanFics, and he gets a few interesting moments in the role. It was sort of a challenge writing him and trying to balance the otaku side of him with his more true personality of the short, scrawny kid who just wants to prove himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Mar 29, 2013 11:38 am Episode four was where Kensuke actually got so be a semi-main character. There was that long scene where Shinji finds him out camping in the fog (and ditching classes, as well), and they wind up talking. A lot. Kensuke's mom is dead, which seems to be a running trend in class 2-A; he also just gets to talk to us for a while without having to provide any sort of comic relief. Well, except for that great(?) scene where he plays out a military drama by himself. That was funny, but in a good way. I'm pretty sure it was something most of us can relate to. While I don't pay as much attention to him as the other characters, episode four definitely lets him shine.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Fri Mar 29, 2013 11:40 am I thought that line was foreshadowing something the first time I watched it. Funny how we get that line from Kensuke, but not from Touji himself...

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Fri Mar 29, 2013 3:32 pm EVAfacepalm wrote: Everyone's voice in the dub is horrible .

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Fri Mar 29, 2013 3:54 pm Atropos wrote: I thought that line was foreshadowing something the first time I watched it. Funny how we get that line from Kensuke, but not from Touji himself...

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Fri Mar 29, 2013 3:58 pm That's not foreshadowing, that's just fuel for the "nobody in this class has a mom" theory.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Fri Mar 29, 2013 11:12 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Episode four was where Kensuke actually got so be a semi-main character. There was that long scene where Shinji finds him out camping in the fog (and ditching classes, as well), and they wind up talking. A lot. Kensuke's mom is dead, which seems to be a running trend in class 2-A; he also just gets to talk to us for a while without having to provide any sort of comic relief. Well, except for that great(?) scene where he plays out a military drama by himself. That was funny, but in a good way. I'm pretty sure it was something most of us can relate to. While I don't pay as much attention to him as the other characters, episode four definitely lets him shine.

[]:             Quote Post by BlackX32 » Mon Apr 01, 2013 8:37 pm Elect G-Max wrote: That's not foreshadowing, that's just fuel for the "nobody in this class has a mom" theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Tue Apr 02, 2013 1:49 am Kensuke doesn't really have any characterization beyond being into military stuff and wanting to pilot an Eva like really badly.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Apr 02, 2013 2:01 am Aiko Heiwa wrote: Kensuke doesn't really have any characterization beyond being into military stuff and wanting to pilot an Eva like really badly.

[]:             Quote Post by Oenara » Tue Apr 02, 2013 2:20 am I found Kensuke a more complex character than Touji and Hikari, who fit more neatly into classic archetypes.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 02, 2013 10:50 am Oenara wrote: I found Kensuke a more complex character than Touji and Hikari, who fit more neatly into classic archetypes.
